description: Plan 7 — Marketplace Readiness. KMS envelope encryption, workspace/user deletion flows, legal pages, scope justification, reviewer sandbox, and production monitoring.

userStories.title: Envelope encryption — KMS-wrapped DEK per workspace
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I need each workspace's tokens encrypted with a per-workspace DEK wrapped by a cloud KMS master key.
acceptanceCriteria: relay/crypto.py extended with generate_dek(), wrap_dek(dek, kms_client), unwrap_dek(wrapped_dek, kms_client) using a KMSProvider abstraction
Migration 0007_plan7_kms.py adds wrapped_dek (LargeBinary) and kms_key_id (String) columns to workspaces
WorkspaceToken, CrmConnection, SourceConnector encryption uses workspace DEK when wrapped_dek is set
Backward compat: if wrapped_dek is null, fall back to global TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY
TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY env var still accepted but marked deprecated in Settings
Unit tests: encrypt/decrypt roundtrip with mocked KMS; fallback path works
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use a KMSProvider abstract base class with a LocalKMSProvider (for tests/dev) that wraps with AES-256 using a local key. Production will use AWS KMS (boto3). The LocalKMSProvider is sufficient for unit tests and CI.

userStories.title: Workspace deletion flow
userStories.description: As a Marketplace reviewer, I need to verify that uninstalling RELAY completely removes all workspace data.
acceptanceCriteria: relay/commands/delete.py implements handle_delete_workspace(ack, client, command, respond) Bolt slash-command handler
Shows confirmation modal: 'This will permanently delete all RELAY data for your workspace. This cannot be undone.'
On modal submission: enqueues delete_workspace_data(workspace_id) Celery task
Migration 0007 adds workspace_deletion_jobs table: id, workspace_id, status (pending/complete/failed), started_at, completed_at
delete_workspace_data task deletes in cascade order per PRD spec
Writes audit_log entry event_type=workspace_deleted before workspace row is deleted
On app_uninstalled Slack event: sets workspace_tokens.is_revoked=True and enqueues deletion job
relay/commands/help.py routes delete-workspace-data subcommand
Unit tests: modal structure correct, deletion task called with correct workspace_id
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The cascade delete uses explicit DELETE queries in dependency order (not Postgres CASCADE) so each step can be logged. Use the existing async session pattern. Check if audit_log table exists or needs to be added in migration 0007.

userStories.title: Connector-level purge
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to disconnect a source connector and purge all derived knowledge.
acceptanceCriteria: relay/slack/settings.py shows a settings view with a Disconnect + Purge button per connected source connector
relay_disconnect_connector action handler shows confirmation modal per connector
On confirm: deletes knowledge_chunks and source_documents for that connector
Sets source_connectors.disconnected_at = now()
Responds ephemeral: '{provider} disconnected. All indexed content removed.'
Unit test: purge deletes chunks and documents, sets disconnected_at
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add a relay_settings slash command or action that opens a settings view. The purge logic can be a standalone async function in relay/connectors/base.py.

userStories.title: Individual user erasure (GDPR Art. 17)
userStories.description: As a data protection officer, I need to erase all data associated with a specific user on request.
acceptanceCriteria: DELETE /relay/admin/users/{slack_user_id}/erase FastAPI endpoint in relay/api/admin.py
Requires relay_role=admin (same Bearer auth as feedback-export) and confirmation_token query param = sha256(slack_user_id + ERASURE_SECRET)
Nullifies PII: users.display_name=null, users.email=null, sets users.deleted_at
Nullifies actor_slack_user_id and actor_user_id on audit_log rows for this user
Nullifies actor_user_id on question_events for this user
Writes audit_log entry event_type=user_erased with admin actor
Does NOT delete questions, assignments, alerts
Returns 200 JSON {erased: true, user_id: slack_user_id}
Unit test: 403 without admin role, PII nullified for admin
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: ERASURE_SECRET env var (default '') in Settings. sha256 confirmation prevents accidental mass erasure. Add ERASURE_SECRET to Settings as an optional field.

userStories.title: Privacy policy, ToS, and sub-processor disclosure pages
userStories.description: As a Marketplace reviewer, I need public legal pages describing data collection, retention, and sub-processors.
acceptanceCriteria: relay/api/legal.py FastAPI router with GET /privacy, GET /terms, GET /sub-processors
Each returns text/html with appropriate legal content
/privacy covers: data collected, retention, sub-processors, user rights, contact
/terms covers: service description, acceptable use, liability, termination
/sub-processors lists: Anthropic (ZDR/no training), embedding provider, hosting, Sentry
All routes accessible without authentication
Router registered in relay/api/app.py
Unit tests: all three routes return 200 non-empty HTML
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Plain HTML with minimal inline CSS. Use settings.relay_contact_email (default 'privacy@relay.app') for contact. Add relay_contact_email to Settings.

userStories.title: Scope justification narrative
userStories.description: As a Marketplace reviewer, I need written justification for every Slack OAuth scope RELAY requests.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/marketplace/scope-justification.md created
One paragraph per scope: groups:history, groups:read, channels:read, chat:write, im:write, users:read, commands
Each paragraph: data accessed, why needed, consequence of denial
Notes: no channels:history, connector scopes are optional
File committed to branch

userStories.title: Reviewer sandbox seed script + walkthrough
userStories.description: As a Slack Marketplace reviewer, I need a sandbox workspace pre-loaded with demo data.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/seed_reviewer_sandbox.py creates: 1 workspace, 2 customer accounts, 2 monitored channels, 3 open questions (one past SLA, one snoozed, one claimed), 1 pending draft, 2 resolved questions with knowledge_entries
Idempotent: re-running resets sandbox (delete-and-recreate by workspace name)
docs/marketplace/reviewer-walkthrough.md documents step-by-step flow
Script accepts --database-url arg; falls back to DATABASE_URL env var
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use SQLAlchemy sync engine for simplicity. Walkthrough: 1-2 sentences per step with expected outputs.

userStories.title: Production monitoring — Sentry + /health checks
userStories.description: As an operator, I need error tracking and uptime monitoring.
acceptanceCriteria: sentry-sdk[fastapi,celery] added to pyproject.toml
relay/api/app.py initializes Sentry if settings.sentry_dsn is set
SENTRY_DSN optional field in Settings (default '')
relay/worker/celery_app.py integrates Sentry celery integration if sentry_dsn set
GET /health extended: checks DB with SELECT 1 in 2s timeout, Redis ping in 1s timeout
Returns {status: ok|degraded, db: ok|error, redis: ok|error, version: str}; 503 if db or redis fail
Unit tests: /health returns 200 with mocked ok deps; Sentry init skipped when SENTRY_DSN empty
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Settings.sentry_dsn: str = ''. /health already exists — extend it. asyncio.wait_for for DB timeout. Get Redis URL from CELERY_BROKER_URL setting.
